goal: 启动 cxs 多源架构改造控制平面

summary.title: 启动 cxs 多源架构控制平面
summary.what: Created a neutral Codex Session Orchestrator control plane for the cxs session-source migration: README, operating rules, live workflow state, registry, milestone plan, task contracts, starter prompts, and handoff directory.
summary.why: The multi-source foundation needs coordinated evidence and bounded implementation sessions before touching parser, selector, coverage, DB, CLI, docs, or skill surfaces. The control plane keeps Codex behavior preservation, Claude Code non-public status, Mainline boundaries, and release/install distinctions explicit.
summary.user_goal: 启动 cxs 多源架构改造控制平面

decisions.chose: Use docs/workflows/2026-06-05-session-sources as the canonical control plane.
decisions.rationale: This keeps the workflow neutral and durable without overloading review or architecture docs.

decisions.chose: Split the work into read-only packets A1/A2/A3, design packet D1, implementation slices I1-I4, evidence E1, and review R1.
decisions.rationale: The source migration crosses parser, inventory, selector, coverage, DB, CLI, docs, and skill boundaries, so independent packets reduce context collision and force decision proof before implementation.

decisions.chose: Do not launch implementation sessions until A1/A2/A3 are reconciled into D1.
decisions.rationale: The high-risk decisions are source identity, selector/coverage semantics, DB migration, and Claude transcript boundaries.

decisions.chose: Keep Claude Code support reserved/experimental and forbid npm release, global skill update, local CLI install, push, PR, or production actions in this workflow unless explicitly authorized later.
decisions.rationale: The user asked for architecture foundation first, not a published Claude adapter or release.

rejected.alternative: Let one long implementation session redesign and edit the full stack immediately.
rejected.reason: That would mix evidence gathering, architecture decisions, DB/CLI changes, and docs updates before source identity and Claude boundaries are proven.

rejected.alternative: Use the original local main checkout for the control plane.
rejected.reason: Mainline preflight showed proposed-overlap noise there, so an isolated worktree from origin/main was safer for ownership and review.

review_notes: Docs-only control-plane slice; no runtime, API, DB, CLI, or skill behavior changed.
Verification performed: git diff --check passed, control-plane file inventory checked, Mainline preflight passed in the isolated worktree.
Session registry intentionally contains TBD thread ids until worker sessions are launched.

architectural_claims: The multi-source migration should be orchestrated through evidence packets before implementation slices.
Codex remains the default public source while Claude Code stays reserved until a later adapter decision.
Release skill, npm CLI release, and local installed CLI state must be reported separately.
security_implications: Worker rules forbid copying sensitive Claude transcript content, secrets, tool outputs, private dogfood goldens, or local credentials into handoffs.
migration_notes: No schema migration in this slice; future migration strategy is assigned to A2/D1/I2.
